utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sophia brown . date of birth , 3/17/1946 . this is a new patient visit . she's here to establish care for a history of dcis . we'll go over the history with the patient .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hello , ms. brown .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hi . yes , that's me .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: wonderful . i'm doctor stewart . it's lovely to meet you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you as well .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , you've come to see me today because you had a right breast lumpectomy last year . is that right ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . on january 20th , 2020 .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and how have you been since then ? any problems or concerns ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i'm feeling good . i do my self breast exams religiously now and have n't felt anything since .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: perfect . i want to back up and go over your history so i can make sure everything in your chart is correct and i do n't miss anything . so , i'll tell you what we have in your chart from your other providers and you tell me if anything is wrong or missing . sound good ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great . so , i have that you were found to have a calcification in your right breast during a mammogram in october 2019 . was that just a normal screening mammogram , or was it done because you felt a lump ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it was just a normal one you're supposed to get every so often .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i see . and then it looks like you had an ultrasound of your right breast on november 3rd , 2019 , which revealed a mass at the two o'clock position , 11 centimeters from the nipple in the retroareolar region . the report states the mass was point four by two by three centimeters .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , that sounds right . hard to remember now , though .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yep , definitely .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: based on those results , they decided to do an ultrasound-guided core needle biopsy on december 5th , 2019 . pathology results during that biopsy came back as grade two , er positive , pr positive , dcis , or ductal carcinoma in situ .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . unfortunately .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i know . scary stuff . but you had a lumpectomy on january 20th , 2020 , which removed the eight millimeter tumor and margins were negative . the pathology confirmed dcis . looks like they also removed 5 lymph nodes , which , thankfully , were negative for malignancy . that's great !

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i was definitely very relieved .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and your last mammogram was in january 2021 ? and that was normal .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so , i feel like i have a good grasp of what's been going on with you now . and you're here today to establish care with me so i can continue to follow you and make sure you're doing well , right ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . fingers crossed .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: definitely . we'll keep a close eye on you and take good care of you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i have a few more questions for you . when was your last colonoscopy ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i had one in 2018 and , if i remember correctly , i had one polyp and that was removed and it was n't cancerous .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , yes , i see that report now . one polyp in the sigmoid colon which had a benign tubular adenoma . okay . and when was your last menstrual period ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: gosh . it was probably around 30 years ago .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . do you have children ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do . i have five .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: ah , big family then . that's nice .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . and they're all having kids of their own now , so it's getting even bigger .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i bet . sounds like fun .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it is .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: did you have any other pregnancies that were miscarriages or terminations ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: really , i did not .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so for the record , that's g5 p5 . and now that you're post-menopausal , are you currently or have you ever been on hormone replacement therapy ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: my primary care doctor gave me the option years ago but i decided against it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and on your review systems form , you indicated that you've not had any recent weight loss or gain , headaches , bone pain , urinary symptoms , or blood in the stools . but you did indicate that you have some back pain , joint pain , and high cholesterol . tell me some more about those .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . so i've seen doctors for all of those . they've said , excuse me , the back and knee pain are age-related . and the cholesterol is a fairly new diagnosis , but i am working on exercise and cutting back on fatty foods to see if i can get it lower without any medication .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and your primary care doctor is following you for that , right ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's correct .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . for medications , i have that you take coq10 , vitamin d , vitamin c , fish oil , and elderberry fruit . is that all right ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , and that's all .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so for your medical history , it's high cholesterol and stage 0 er/pr positive invasive ductal carcinoma of the right breast . any surgeries other than the lumpectomy ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i did have my tubes tied after my last baby , but that's all .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and how about family history ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: my mom had non-hodgkin's lymphoma and my dad had prostate cancer and heart disease , but i think that's it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . any family history of breast cancer ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: none .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: did any of your children have medical issues or siblings with medical problems ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do not have any siblings and , thankfully , my children are all healthy .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: wonderful . do you have any history of smoking , illicit drug use , heavy alcohol consumption ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no drugs . i do drink socially , but never more than that . and i used to smoke , but really , everybody did back then and i probably quit about 30 years ago .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: excellent . i have that you're allergic to penicillin . any other allergies ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nope , just penicillin .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . i think that covers it . hop up here and let me take a look at you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so let's use the normal new patient exam template . only change to make is the breast exam . there are no palpable masses , however , there is skin thickening at the medial inferior aspect of the right breast which may be radiation skin changes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: in the result section , note that her ecog performance status today is zero .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do you have ... did you have radiation after the lumpectomy ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i did . we also talked about endocrine therapy , but i decided against that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so your exam looks good , no masses , just some skin changes from that radiation . now , let's go over the plan for you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: as you know , you've had dcis which we'll list in my note as stage zero , er/pr positive , invasive ductal carcinoma of the right breast . your status post-lumpectomy with removal of five lymph nodes that were benign . you also had , um , radiation therapy but declined endocrine therapy . today's clinical examination shows no evidence of recurrence with the dcis or other malignancy and your mammogram in january , 2021 was also negative for recurrence and malignancy .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , based on all of that , we can just continue to observe you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . that sounds great . and when do i come back in to see you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: in a year , but you should have another mammogram in april of 2022 before you come back to see me .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , i can do that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: wonderful . i'm glad to see you doing so well . do you have any questions or concerns i can address for you today ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do n't think so .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . my nurse will be in shortly to discharge you . take care !

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you as well .

detail_list.summary: Sophia Brown is a 75-year-old female presenting for a new patient evaluation for a history of right DCIS.
Right breast lumpectomy history.
The doctor asks if the patient has any problems or concerns since her surgery.
The patient reports feeling well and performs regular self-breast exams.
The doctor asks if the October 2019 mammogram was a routine screening or prompted by a lump.
The patient confirms the mammogram was a routine screening.
The patient had a right breast ultrasound on 11/03/2019 showing a 0.4 x 2.0 x 3.0 cm mass at the 2 o'clock position.
The patient underwent an ultrasound-guided core needle biopsy on 12/05/2019, which revealed grade 2 ER-positive, PR-positive DCIS.
The patient had a lumpectomy on 01/20/2020 for an 8 mm tumor with negative margins and 5 benign lymph nodes.
The doctor asks if the patient's last mammogram was in January 2021 and if it was normal.
The patient confirms her January 2021 mammogram was normal.
Establish care for continued monitoring.
The doctor asks when the patient's last colonoscopy was.
The patient had a colonoscopy in 2018 with a benign sigmoid polyp removed.
The doctor asks when the patient's last menstrual period was.
The patient's last menstrual period was approximately 30 years ago.
The doctor asks if the patient has children.
The doctor asks about history of miscarriages or terminations.
The patient denies history of miscarriages or terminations.
The doctor asks if the patient is currently or has ever been on hormone replacement therapy.
The patient declined hormone replacement therapy in the past.
The doctor asks for more information regarding the patient's back pain, joint pain, and high cholesterol.
The patient reports back and knee pain are age-related and she is managing high cholesterol with exercise and diet.
The doctor asks if the patient's primary care provider is following her for high cholesterol.
The patient confirms her primary care provider is following her for high cholesterol.
The doctor asks to confirm the patient's current supplements.
The patient confirms she takes Co-Q 10, Vitamin D, Vitamin C, fish oil, and elderberry fruit.
The doctor asks if the patient has had any surgeries other than the lumpectomy.
The patient had a bilateral tubal ligation.
The doctor asks about family history.
The patient reports mother had non-Hodgkin's lymphoma and father had prostate cancer and heart disease.
The doctor asks about family history of breast cancer.
The patient denies family history of breast cancer.
The doctor asks about medical issues in children or siblings.
The patient has no siblings and her children are healthy.
The doctor asks about history of smoking, illicit drug use, or heavy alcohol consumption.
The patient is a former smoker who quit 30 years ago, denies illicit drug use, and drinks alcohol socially.
The doctor asks about allergies.
The patient has a penicillin allergy.
Physical exam shows no palpable breast masses, but skin thickening at the medial inferior aspect of the right breast is noted.
The patient's ECOG performance status is grade 0.
The doctor asks if the patient had radiation after the lumpectomy.
The patient confirms she had radiation therapy but declined endocrine therapy.
The plan is to continue observation.
The patient asks when she should return for follow-up.
The patient is to have a mammogram in April 2022 and follow up with the doctor in one year.
The doctor asks if the patient has any further questions or concerns.

case_description: right ductal carcinoma in situ; none
